summary: This cluster of research focuses on the taxonomic revision of the diatom genus *Amphora sensu lato*, integrating molecular phylogenetics with detailed morphological analysis to resolve evolutionary relationships. A key finding was the phylogenetic distinction of the subgenus *Halamphora Cleve*, supporting its reclassification as a separate genus, and the identification of *Amphora terroris* as a new species. The studies collectively emphasize that morphological data remain critical for validating taxonomic boundaries within this globally distributed group, which inhabits diverse marine and estuarine environments.
